generatedSentence: Elias Thorne, a seasoned cyberpunk investigator, felt a familiar weakness in his old knee, a reminder of a past injure. His fierce independence was legendary, but a cryptic telegram from an unknown supervisor made him panicky. The errand felt marginal, almost a clownish distraction, a deceptive capot. He watched the aurora streaming over the heather outside his chamber, the thermal glow reflecting off his feline-like eyes. He'd been a wayward youth, his true infancy marred by hardship, yet it had forged a knightly sense of purpose. Now, a strange beetle-shaped device hinted at a molecular scheme, a web of deceit he had to bracket. He knew this wasn't merely a charity case; it required the cunning of a weasel to navigate. The next morning, Elias was summoned to a grand library, its towering shelves resembling an ornate birdcage. His contact, a man of immense lordship, greeted him with patronizing chuckles. The target was the Lord's nephew, a man known for his untamed rage and a past carjack incident. Elias reckoned with the force he might need, his efforts to remain healthy strained. He noticed a peculiar sticker on a rare volume, glowing faintly as if infused with an arcane isotope. The scent of lemon and apricot from a nearby vase offered a fleeting sense of luxury, but Elias felt like an anaconda coiling, ready to strike. He had to unravel the intricate connections, the coins of corruption that bought loyalty, an almost impermeable network. He realized this operation required him to bracket any personal feelings, separating the electron from the atom. A distinctive necklace was described as key to the scheme, and the room had a strange glycerin-like sheen. Elias traced the jailbird nephew to a hidden sanatorium near a murky bayou, a place of sinister reputation. The air smelled of sweet jasmine, masking decay. The nephew, a greedy man on a warpath to seize a legendary diamond, had several henchmen. Elias feels the tension rise, his internal sonar tracking every movement. He needed to randomize his approach, creating a twister of chaos. With a sudden jumps, he disabled a guard, then used a specialized toggle device, designed by a master turner, to disrupt the security. He heard the faint wail of a saxophone from inside, a morbid soundtrack provided by the resident undertaker. This place was a polymer of madness and ambition, a nationwide conspiracy being repackaged for a global market. He was on the longe line, observing the escalating entropy. A distant firework display flashed, momentarily silhouetting a decorative sickle on the wall as Elias was beckoned by a shadowy figure. The shadowy figure revealed itself as the true mastermind, a woman of unyielding resolve and regal majesty, whose eyebrow lifted in amusement. She offered Elias an alternative, a jackpot of interstellar data and ultimate power within this dark society. He felt his internal compass tickled by the temptation, but his purpose remained clear. He had to deliver a knockout blow to her empire. He realized her global influence was like a vast refinery processing information, her views shaping the world. He observed her minions, who flocked around her like a harpy's brood. With a deceptive move, like a jackrabbit darting through ragweed, Elias seized the keys to a critical data vault. His efforts, though seemingly insufficient against such a powerful foe, were made with a willing heart. He vividly recalled his past as a journeyman, navigating the murky water of the underworld, his resolve vaunted. He finally apprehended the mysterious nymph-like associate from the cantina, securing his victory.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, neo-noir graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Protagonist is a grizzled, middle-aged man with a sharp, angular face, five o'clock shadow, and a worn leather trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elias Thorne, a middle-aged man with a sharp, angular face, looking intently at a glowing beetle-shaped device on a cluttered desk, in his dim, cyberpunk chamber with an aurora visible through the window. Close-up, slightly low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elias Thorne, his face showing subtle tension, standing in a vast, opulent library, discreetly observing a younger but visibly adult man (the nephew) wearing a distinctive necklace, while a faint glow emanates from a sticker on a nearby ancient book. Medium shot, eye-level. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elias Thorne, an adult man with a determined expression, silently disabling a large, visibly adult guard with a toggle device in a dilapidated sanatorium hallway, a faint saxophone silhouette in the background. Dynamic action shot, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elias Thorne, a victorious, weathered man, holding a set of keys, stands over a formidable, mature woman (the mastermind) whose expression is one of grudging respect, in a high-tech data vault. Wide shot, slightly high angle. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue, NO children.

definitions.definition: The state or quality of being independent; freedom from dependence; exemption from reliance on, or control by others; self-subsistence or maintenance; direction of one's own affairs without interference.
The state of having sufficient means for a comfortable livelihood.
To wound or cause physical harm to a living creature.
To damage or impair.
To do injustice to.
A message transmitted by telegraph.
To send a telegram.
To send a telegram to (a person).
To send (a message) in a telegram.
A person with the official task of overseeing the work of a person or group, or of other operations and activities.
A person who monitors someone to make sure they comply with rules or other requirements set for them.
In certain states, an elected member of the governing body for a county which is called the board of supervisors.
A process responsible for managing other processes.
A small car, the Volkswagen Beetle (original version made 1938–2003, similar models made 1997–2010 and since 2011)
Any of numerous species of insect in the order Coleoptera characterized by a pair of hard, shell-like front wings which cover and protect a pair of rear wings when at rest.
A game of chance in which players attempt to complete a drawing of a beetle, different dice rolls allowing them to add the various body parts.
To move away quickly, to scurry away.
definitions.example: He beetled off on his vacation.

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to a knight or knights.
Befitting a knight; formally courteous (as a knight); chivalrous, gallant and courtly.
definitions.example: knightly combat

definitions.definition: In the manner of a knight; chivalrously.
In a state of panic.

An atmospheric phenomenon created by charged particles from the sun striking the upper atmosphere, creating coloured lights in the sky. It is usually named australis or borealis based on whether it is in the Southern or Northern Hemisphere respectively.
An evergreen plant, Calluna vulgaris, with spiky leaves and small purple, pink, or white flowers.
The Ericaceae family.
Various species of the genus Erica.
Various species of the genus Cassiope.
A purple colour with a tint of pink and blue.
The use of interwoven yarns of mixed colours to produce flecks.
Of a purple colour with a tint with pink and blue.
A winning of all the tricks in the game of piquet, counting for forty points.
To win all the tricks (from), when playing at piquet.
Like a circus clown; comical, ridiculous.
Pertaining to peasants; rustic.
Uncultured, boorish; rough, coarse.
A subgenre of science fiction which focuses on computer or information technology and virtual reality juxtaposed with a degree of breakdown or radical change in the social order.
A cyberpunk character, a hacker punk, a high-tech low life.
definitions.example: The film “The Matrix” redefined what a cyberpunk looked like.

definitions.definition: A writer of cyberpunk fiction.
A musical genre related to the punk movement that makes use of electronic sounds such as synthesizers.
An organization, the objective of which is to carry out a charitable purpose.
The goods or money given to those in need.
Benevolence to others less fortunate than ourselves; the providing of goods or money to those in need.
In general, an attitude of kindness and understanding towards others, now especially suggesting generosity.
Christian love; representing God's love of man, man's love of God, or man's love of his fellow-men.
A room or set of rooms, particularly:
A chamberpot.
The legislature or division of the legislature itself.
definitions.example: The resolution, which speedily passed the Senate, was unable to gain a majority in the lower chamber.

definitions.definition: Any enclosed space occupying or similar to a room.
definitions.example: A canal lock chamber; a furnace chamber; a test chamber

definitions.definition: An enlarged space in an underground tunnel of a burrowing animal.
The area holding the ammunition round at the initiation of its discharge.
definitions.example: Dianne loaded a cartridge into the chamber of the rifle, then prepared to take aim at the target.

definitions.definition: One of the bullet-holding compartments in the cylinder of a revolver.
A short piece of ordnance or cannon which stood on its breech without any carriage, formerly used chiefly for celebrations and theatrical cannonades.
One of the two atria or two ventricles of the heart.
To enclose in a room.
definitions.example: She had chambered herself in her room, and wouldn't come out.

definitions.definition: To reside in or occupy a chamber or chambers.
To place in a chamber, as a round of ammunition.
definitions.example: The hunter fired at the geese and missed, then shrugged his shoulders and chambered another cartridge.

definitions.definition: To create or modify a gun to be a specific caliber.
definitions.example: The rifle was originally chambered for 9mm, but had since been modified for a larger, wildcat caliber.

definitions.definition: In martial arts, to prepare an offensive, defensive, or counteroffensive action by drawing a limb or weapon to a position where it may be charged with kinetic energy.
definitions.example: Bob chambered his fist for a blow, but Sheila struck first.

definitions.definition: To be lascivious.
A container used for urination and defecation, particularly those used in early modern towns before the advent of the flush toilet.
The condition of being weak.
definitions.example: In a small number of horses, muscle weakness may progress to paralysis.

definitions.definition: An inadequate quality; fault
definitions.example: His inability to speak in front of an audience was his weakness.

definitions.definition: A special fondness or desire.
definitions.example: She is an athlete who has a weakness for chocolate.

definitions.definition: Given to wilful, perverse deviation from the expected norm; tending to stray
Obstinate, contrary and unpredictable
Not on target
The least weasel, Mustela nivalis.
Any of the carnivorous mammals of the genus Mustela, having a slender body, a long tail and usually a light brown upper coat and light-coloured belly.
The taxonomic family Mustelidae is also called the weasel family.
A devious or sneaky person or animal.

A type of yarn winder used for counting the yardage of handspun yarn. It most commonly has a wooden peg or dowel that pops up from the gearing mechanism after a certain number of yards have been wound onto the winder.
To achieve by clever or devious means.
To gain something for oneself by clever or devious means.
To engage in clever or devious behavior.
Something that is marginal.
A constituency won with a small margin.
Of, relating to, or located at or near a margin or edge; also figurative usages of location and margin (edge).
definitions.example: In recent years there has been an increase in violence against marginal groups.

definitions.definition: Determined by a small margin; having a salient characteristic determined by a small margin.
Pertaining to changes resulting from a unit increase in production or consumption of a good.
Any compound having a specified (range of) molecular weight(s)
Relating to, or consisting of, or produced by molecules.
(of an element) Combined with itself and with no other element; elemental.
Relating to a simple or basic structure or organization.
Relating to molecular gastronomy.
definitions.example: molecular dishes

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to cats.
Catlike (resembling a cat)
A journey undertaken to accomplish some task.
The purpose of such a journey.
An oral message trusted to a person for delivery.
To send someone on an errand.
definitions.example: All the servants were on holiday or erranded out of the house.

definitions.definition: To go on an errand.
definitions.example: She spent an enjoyable afternoon erranding in the city.

definitions.definition: The earliest period of childhood (crawling rather than walking).
The state of being an infant.
An early stage in the development of anything.
definitions.example: Space tourism is still in its infancy.

definitions.definition: The state of being a minor.
A column of rising air in the lower atmosphere created by uneven heating of Earth's surface.
(stone) To create a rough finish on stone by treating it with a high-temperature blow-torch.
(gliding, often in the present participle) To fly an unpowered aircraft in a (thermal) column of rising air.
Pertaining to heat or temperature.
(fabric) Providing efficient insulation so as to keep the body warm.
Caused or brought about by heat.
(stone) Having a rough finish by treatment with a blow-torch.
To flow in a continuous or steady manner, like a liquid.
To extend; to stretch out with a wavy motion; to float in the wind.
definitions.example: A flag streams in the wind.

definitions.definition: To discharge in a stream.
definitions.example: The soldier's wound was streaming blood.

definitions.definition: To push continuous data (e.g. music) from a server to a client computer while it is being used (played) on the client.
Flowing or moving in continuous succession, like fluid in a stream.
A fixture attached to a wall to hold up a shelf.
Any intermediate object that connects a smaller part to a larger part, the smaller part typically projecting sideways from the larger part.
A short crooked timber, resembling a knee, used as a support.
The cheek or side of an ordnance carriage, supporting the trunnions.
Any of the characters "(", ")", "[", "]", "{", "}", "<" and ">", used in pairs to enclose parenthetic remarks, sections of mathematical expressions, etc.
A printed diagram of games in a tournament.
A prediction of the outcome of games in a tournament, used for betting purposes.
One of several ranges of numbers.
definitions.example: tax bracket, age bracket

definitions.definition: A pair of values that represent the smallest and largest elements of a range.
In artillery, the endangered region between two shell impacts (one long and one short). The next shell fired is likely to hit accurately.
The small curved or angular corner formed by a serif and a stroke in a letter.
(land surveying, 19th century) a mark cut into a stone by land surveyors to secure a bench.
To support by means of mechanical brackets.
To enclose in typographical brackets.
To bound on both sides, to surround, as enclosing with brackets.
definitions.example: I tried to hit the bullseye by first bracketing it with two shots and then splitting the difference with my third, but I missed.

definitions.definition: To place in the same category.
definitions.example: Because the didn't have enough young boys for two full teams, they bracketed the seven-year olds with the eight-year olds.

definitions.definition: To mark distinctly for special treatment.
To set aside, discount, ignore.
To take multiple images of the same subject, using a range of exposure settings, in order to help ensure that a satisfactory image is obtained.

(phenomenology) In the philosophical system of Edmund Husserl and his followers, to set aside metaphysical theories and existential questions concerning what is real in order to focus philosophical attention simply on the actual content of experience.

definitions.example: tax bracket, age bracket

definitions.example: Because the didn't have enough young boys for two full teams, they bracketed the seven-year olds with the eight-year olds.

definitions.definition: To mark distinctly for special treatment.
To set aside, discount, ignore.
To take multiple images of the same subject, using a range of exposure settings, in order to help ensure that a satisfactory image is obtained.

(phenomenology) In the philosophical system of Edmund Husserl and his followers, to set aside metaphysical theories and existential questions concerning what is real in order to focus philosophical attention simply on the actual content of experience.
A cage to keep pet or zoological specimen birds in.
A game of chance played with dice; chuck-a-luck.
A round sweet and juicy stone fruit, resembling peach or plum in taste, with a yellow-orange flesh, lightly fuzzy skin and a large seed inside.
The apricot tree, Prunus armeniaca
A pale yellow-orange colour, like that of an apricot fruit.
A dog with an orange-coloured coat.
The junction of the brain and brain stem on a target, used as an aiming point to ensure a one-shot kill.
(usually in the plural) A testicle.
Of a pale yellowish-orange colour, like that of an apricot.
An institution which holds books and/or other forms of media for use by the public or qualified people often lending them out, as well as providing various other services for its users.
(by extension) Any institution that lends out its goods for use by the public or a community.
A collection of books or other forms of stored information.
An equivalent collection of analogous information in a non-printed form, e.g. record library.
A room dedicated to storing books.
A collection of software routines that provide functionality to be incorporated into or used by a computer program.
A collection of DNA material from a single organism or relative to a single disease.
The deck or draw pile.
The state or condition of being a lord.
(hence, with "his" or "your", often capitalised) Title applied to a lord, bishop, judge, or another man with a title.
definitions.example: May I ask that the order be granted, if your lordship so pleases?

definitions.definition: (with "his" or "your") A boy or man who is behaving in a seigneurial manner or acting like a lord, behaving in a bossy manner or lording it up
Seigniory; domain; the territory over which a lord holds jurisdiction; a manor.
Dominion; power; authority.
Not giving in; not bending; stubborn.
Wild, uncontrolled, especially of animals not domesticated or trained to human contact.
definitions.example: The mustang is an untamed horse that roams where it wants, with little interest in humans.

definitions.definition: A quiet laugh.
To steal an automobile forcibly from (someone).
definitions.example: Someone should carjack that pompous jerk and teach him a lesson!

definitions.definition: To forcibly steal (a vehicle).
definitions.example: These twerps have carjacked their last Mercedes.

definitions.definition: To count; to enumerate; to number; also, to compute; to calculate.
To count as in a number, rank, or series; to estimate by rank or quality; to place by estimation; to account; to esteem; to repute.
To charge, attribute, or adjudge to one, as having a certain quality or value.
To conclude, as by an enumeration and balancing of chances; hence, to think; to suppose; -- followed by an objective clause
definitions.example: I reckon he won't try that again.

definitions.definition: To reckon with something or somebody or not, i.e to reckon without something or somebody: to take into account, deal with, consider or not, i.e. to misjudge, ignore, not take into account, not deal with, not consider or fail to consider; e.g. reckon without one's host
To make an enumeration or computation; to engage in numbering or computing.
To come to an accounting; to draw up or settle accounts; to examine and strike the balance of debt and credit; to adjust relations of desert or penalty.
An article of jewelry that is worn around the neck, most often made of a string of precious metal, pearls, gems, beads or shells, and sometimes having a pendant attached.
Anything resembling a necklace in shape.
definitions.example: a necklace of coral islands

definitions.definition: A device used in necklacing (an informal execution); a rubber tyre that is filled with petrol. It is placed around the victim's chest and arms, and set on fire.
To informally execute by necklacing; by setting on fire a petrol-filled rubber tyre which has been put around the bound victim's neck.
A son of one's sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either a son of one's brother (fraternal nephew) or a son of one's sister (sororal nephew).
A son of one's child.
The part of the day from dawn to noon.
definitions.example: I'll see you tomorrow morning.

definitions.definition: The part of the day between midnight and noon.
The early part of anything.
The first alcoholic drink of the day; a morning draught.
A greeting said in the morning; shortening of good morning
The common name for glycerol, glycerin or E422.
Strength or energy of body or mind; active power; vigour; might; capacity of exercising an influence or producing an effect.
definitions.example: the force of an appeal, an argument, or a contract

definitions.definition: Power exerted against will or consent; compulsory power; violence; coercion.
Anything that is able to make a substantial change in a person or thing.

A physical quantity that denotes ability to push, pull, twist or accelerate a body and which has a direction and is measured in a unit dimensioned in mass × distance/time² (ML/T²): SI: newton (N); CGS: dyne (dyn)
Something or anything that has the power to produce a physical effect upon something else, such as causing it to move or change shape.
A group that aims to attack, control, or constrain.
definitions.example: police force

definitions.definition: The ability to attack, control, or constrain.
definitions.example: show of force

definitions.definition: A magic trick in which the outcome is known to the magician beforehand, especially one involving the apparent free choice of a card by another person.
Legal validity.
definitions.example: The law will come into force in January.

definitions.definition: Either unlawful violence, as in a "forced entry", or lawful compulsion.
Ability of an utterance or its element (word, form, prosody, ...) to effect a given meaning.
(with the, often capitalized) A metaphysical and ubiquitous power from the fictional Star Wars universe created by George Lucas. See usage note.
To violate (a woman); to rape.
To exert oneself, to do one's utmost.
To compel (someone or something) to do something.
To constrain by force; to overcome the limitations or resistance of.
To drive (something) by force, to propel (generally + prepositional phrase or adverb).
To cause to occur (despite inertia, resistance etc.); to produce through force.
definitions.example: The comedian's jokes weren't funny, but I forced a laugh now and then.

definitions.definition: To forcibly open (a door, lock etc.).
definitions.example: To force a lock.

definitions.definition: To obtain or win by strength; to take by violence or struggle; specifically, to capture by assault; to storm, as a fortress.
To create an out by touching a base in advance of a runner who has no base to return to while in possession of a ball which has already touched the ground.
definitions.example: Jones forced the runner at second by stepping on the bag.

definitions.definition: (whist) To compel (an adversary or partner) to trump a trick by leading a suit that he/she does not hold.
To put in force; to cause to be executed; to make binding; to enforce.
To provide with forces; to reinforce; to strengthen by soldiers; to man; to garrison.

definitions.definition: Strictly thermodynamic entropy. A measure of the amount of energy in a physical system that cannot be used to do work.
A measure of the disorder present in a system.
definitions.example: Ludwig Boltzmann defined entropy as being directly proportional to the natural logarithm of the number of microstates yielding an equivalent thermodynamic macrostate (with the eponymous constant of proportionality). Assuming (by the fundamental postulate of statistical mechanics), that all microstates are equally probable, this means, on the one hand, that macrostates with higher entropy are more probable, and on the other hand, that for such macrostates, the quantity of information required to describe a particular one of its microstates will be higher. That is, the Shannon entropy of a macrostate would be directly proportional to the logarithm of the number of equivalent microstates (making it up). In other words, thermodynamic and informational entropies are rather compatible, which shouldn't be surprising since Claude Shannon derived the notation 'H' for information entropy from Boltzmann's H-theorem.

definitions.definition: The capacity factor for thermal energy that is hidden with respect to temperature http//arxiv.org/pdf/physics/0004055.

The dispersal of energy; how much energy is spread out in a process, or how widely spread out it becomes, at a specific temperature. https//web.archive.org/web/20060702234316/http://www.entropysite.com/students_approach.html

To attune to; to set at; to pitch.
To fasten or secure firmly; to fasten or tighten with keys or wedges.
One of a string of small islands.
definitions.example: the Florida Keys

definitions.definition: In the International System of Units, the base unit of mass; conceived of as the mass of one litre of water, but now defined by taking the fixed numerical value of the Planck constant h to be 6.626 070 15 × 10-34 when expressed in units of kg⋅m2⋅s−1. Symbol: kg
The unit of weight such that a one-kilogram mass is also a one-kilogram weight.
A stone or concrete structure on navigable water used for loading and unloading vessels; a wharf.
A situation which allows a mutually exclusive choice between two or more possibilities; a choice between two or more possibilities.
One of several mutually exclusive things which can be chosen.
The remaining option; something available after other possibilities have been exhausted.
Alternative rock
Relating to a choice between two or more possibilities.
definitions.example: an alternative proposition

definitions.definition: Other; different from something else.
Not traditional, outside the mainstream, underground.
Alternate, reciprocal.
A fabulous winged monster, ravenous and filthy, having the face of a woman and the body of a vulture.
A shrewish woman.
One who is rapacious or ravenous; an extortioner.
The European moor buzzard or marsh harrier (Circus aeruginosus).
A large and powerful double-crested, short-winged American eagle (Harpia harpyja).
A drinking establishment, often specifically of the type found in Latin America.
Abbreviated form of cantina truck, cantina wagon or cantina trailer
To wish, desire.
To instruct (that something be done) in one's will.
To try to make (something) happen by using one's will (intention).
definitions.example: All the fans were willing their team to win the game.

definitions.definition: To bequeath (something) to someone in one's will (legal document).
definitions.example: He willed his stamp collection to the local museum.

definitions.definition: The execution of a will.
Ready to do something that is not (can't be expected as) a matter of course.
definitions.example: If my boyfriend isn't willing to change his drinking habits, I will split up with him.

definitions.definition: In a vivid manner.
(physical) Visual perception.
A picture, drawn or painted; a sketch.
definitions.example: a fine view of Lake George

definitions.definition: (psychological) Opinion, judgement, imagination.
A virtual or logical table composed of the result set of a query in relational databases.
The part of a computer program which is visible to the user and can be interacted with
A wake.
To look at.
definitions.example: The video was viewed by millions of people.

definitions.definition: To regard in a stated way.
definitions.example: I view it as a serious breach of trust.
